questions.question: What's the actual impact of fees and slippage on retail-size paper-traded crypto strategies?
questions.summary: ~$1/round-trip on $200 positions (0.25% taker both legs). Gross +$1,085 became net -$782 on the 3-month backtest — the delta is the answer. Fees erase thin edges.

questions.question: Which parameter ranges of Bollinger and MA-crossover are robust to regime shifts, and which are overfit?
questions.summary: None are robust: all 12 variants with n>100 negative over 6 months net of costs. Per-regime conditioning untested (rung 3).

questions.question: (Emergent) Do constraints or strategies determine outcomes at this scale?
questions.summary: Constraints dominate: 99.6% of candidate signals died on cooldown/exposure/concurrency. Candidate fitness = edge per constraint slot, not signal frequency.

questions.question: Does sentiment data (Fear & Greed) improve technical strategies, or is it noise?
questions.summary: Layer 2 (context) never went live; deferred past Phase 1b per one-factor-at-a-time.

questions.question: Does the walk-forward tuner produce variants that hold up out-of-sample?
questions.summary: tune.py never built; compare.py (the scoring instrument) now exists. Blocked on the candidate roster accumulating live trades.

learnings.claim: GitHub Actions free-tier */5 cron delivers only ~3-5% of scheduled invocations for 5-minute schedules on small/inactive public repos, not the documented 70-85%.
learnings.evidence: 2026-05-23 migration entry (69 of 1,712 = 4.0% invocation rate) and 2026-05-18 entry (observed ~60-min cadence vs documented 5-min)

learnings.claim: Migrating cron to a Hetzner CX22 VPS clears the 95%-uptime architectural gate as written, without any gate amendment.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 47-day-gap entry: runs 84 -> 11,574 all status='ok', 11,490+ commits with zero gaps; Phase 2 gate 1 mechanically satisfied

learnings.claim: Hetzner blocks outbound port 465 but leaves 587 open, so digest email must go via SMTP 587/STARTTLS.
The claude.ai Gmail connector is draft-only and cannot send email.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 'Seam closed for real' entry (cloud digest-mailer routine disabled as a result)

learnings.claim: Both base textbook strategies (Bollinger mean-reversion and MA-crossover) fail to clear the bar over a real 6-month window; every variant with n>100 is net-negative.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 'Retire Bollinger and MA-crossover' entry: 12 variants, net -$1,719.44 (-0.13%), bollinger_default worst at n=539/-$636.75

learnings.claim: bollinger_loose (sigma=2.5) fired zero times across all six months, meaning it is too wide as a rule, not merely too wide for one regime.
Registering all 12 variants in parallel on Day 1 adds no exposure risk because variants share a single $1,000 position ceiling and compete for the same cap.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 'Front-loaded all 12 variants' entry: 99.6% of candidate signals rejected, total exposure never breached

learnings.claim: All five LLM-surfaced candidates lose money net of fees, replicating the fee-floor finding on novel strategies.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 gauntlet-results entry (reviews/candidates-2026-07-16.md, reports/gauntlet-2026-07-16.md): all five negative, ranked -$0.21 to -$2.56/trade

learnings.claim: weekend_illiquidity_momentum is gross-positive (win rate 49.4%, gross Sharpe 1.51) but fee-negative — the edge is real but smaller than the ~0.6% round-trip cost.

Maker fills beat taker fills in cost, winning 12/12 matched pairs (mean +$0.14/slot, ~$0.21-0.24 fees saved/trade) with near-zero fill loss because the drawdown-buying strategy has price falling into the resting limit.
The first net-positive configuration (maker/72h/tp8%/sl4% at +$0.031/slot over 146 trades) has a statistically-zero edge — best of 24 cells, inside noise, likely negative true expectation.
The gauntlet's detection power is near zero where interesting edges live: a true +0.17%-net edge passes only 32% at n=100 and less with more data; reliable detection starts ~+0.6% net.
The +0.3% kill bar has a 21% false-positive rate at n=7, quantitatively vindicating the n<30 quarantine rule.
First venue measurements show Alpaca crypto spreads of 0.07% (BTC) to 0.42% (SOL) versus Binance <=0.015%, meaning fees+spread are selectable and only slippage requires real trades to measure.
Ten ideas died in the same corner of design space (short-horizon taker-fee mean-reversion), so searching a structurally different space beats sampling the old one harder.
No real credential was ever committed to the repo; the May 2026 .env exposure surface was the session transcript, not the repository.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 post-incident entry (full git-history search 2026-07-16 confirms .env never tracked, only placeholders in .env.template)

learnings.claim: Nearly everything the project has learned was produced in the ~48 hours of 2026-07-16/17; the ~80 days of elapsed calendar time bought only one day of live trade-days, the sole incompressible resource.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 'Correction: the sequence was valuable; the duration was not' entry (live clock started 2026-07-16)

learnings.claim: A monitoring system with nobody reading its output is not a substitute for operator engagement — 47 operator-absent days moved signals/trades/decisions/llm_calls all at zero while VPS uptime accrued.
null_baseline determinism (hash of symbol+bar_timestamp plus a UNIQUE constraint) is required because random() would double-signal on re-runs and make backtests unreproducible.
Growing LENSES from 5 to 8 silently pushed the synthesis output past max_tokens, and the truncated tool input validated as {} and died with a misleading 'field missing' error.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 'Seam closed for real' entry (fixes: lens rotation, max_tokens 16384, raise on stop_reason=max_tokens)

learnings.claim: The blind-elicitation exercise produced more validated learning in one day than any week of inside work (ceiling study, power calibration, netting insight, slow-band prior, cost decomposition all trace to it).
The fixed-n promotion gate (p<0.05 at 100+ closes) has measured power of only ~20-25%.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 'Four rulings from the learning-maximization review' entry, point 3

learnings.claim: The combination-stage prize is netting (disagreeing signals -> no trade; offsets -> cost never paid), not sqrt-breadth diversification, and the netting ratio is computable offline from persisted trade records before any machinery is built.
The meta-conclusion '5-min OHLCV has no edge' is only supportable as 'no edge >= ~0.6% net detectable by this instrument'; smaller edges remain open.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of the first calendar week of cron operation, the runs table should contain ~2,016 rows with status='ok' (90%+ success rate); if below 90%, investigate before Week 2 strategy-roster review.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-17 — Curriculum anchor is first cron run
hypotheses.resolution: Falsified by 2026-05-18/2026-05-23 findings: GitHub Actions delivered only ~4% invocation rate (69 of 1,712), far below the assumed cadence; the original hypothesis was amended on 2026-05-23 to separate invocation rate from code success rate.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, the operator commits a decision-log entry naming one of scheduler options (a/b/c); if no decision is committed by end of Week 2, the scheduler issue becomes the project's most expensive un-made call and the Week 3 build does not begin until it's made.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-18 — GH cron cadence does not match assumed 5-min
hypotheses.resolution: Effectively resolved by the 2026-05-23 decision to migrate the cron to a Hetzner VPS, which cleared the scheduler question.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, Claude-mediated synthesis will produce either (a) a defensible justification for keeping Bollinger and MA-crossover naming which modern-filter criteria they pass/fail, or (b) a defensible alternative roster with the same justification structure; if neither, the LLM-as-epistemological-backbone thesis is weaker than expected and Week 3 starts a postmortem on the reframe.
hypotheses.resolution: The 2026-07-02 entry retired Bollinger and MA-crossover on 6-month evidence rather than at end of Week 2; per the 2026-07-02 correction entry, the Week 2 operator session never happened and the retire call came ~10 weeks later on empirical replay, not the committed 2-week synthesis timeline.

hypotheses.commitment: Original: 90%+ code success rate by end of the first calendar week of GitHub-Actions-invoked runs.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-17 — Curriculum anchor is first cron run
hypotheses.resolution: Amended on 2026-05-23: the platform-induced invocation ceiling made the original single-metric hypothesis unfalsifiable; replaced by two separately-tracked metrics (invocation rate >=99%, code success rate >=95%).

hypotheses.commitment: If neither base strategy clears the bar over a real window, replace them (the reframe's falsifiable hypothesis on the strategy roster).
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-04-26 — Claude as epistemological backbone (project reframe) / Week 2 roster review
hypotheses.resolution: The 2026-07-02 6-month replay showed every n>100 variant net-negative; both base strategies retired from the active roster.

hypotheses.commitment: Program-level kill criterion v2: by 2026-08-31, and every 6 months thereafter, run the ceiling study per a locked analysis plan and evaluate the futility trigger (one-sided 90% upper CB of net_edge below zero at every horizon in H).
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-07-18 — Critique-driven reorder: extraction engineering, conditional program

hypotheses.commitment: null_baseline (the placebo arm) is expected to show slowly-negative P&L; if it shows a positive edge over a real sample, something is wrong with the simulator, fee model, or market data — investigate before believing any candidate's results.

rejection_streak_gated_ignition (r002) gets an out-of-sample re-test on the NEXT 180d window before any gate-family idea is judged again; that result decides whether the gate fits the current regime or best-of-round is what recency overfit looks like.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-07-17 — Seam closed for real; first fully autonomous cycle ran

hypotheses.commitment: Phase 2 drawdown exit: real money returns to paper (or project archives) when the account drops below $700 (-30%) — stop, postmortem.
Phase 2 variance exit: real diverges from paper by >50% over any 2-week window triggers a halt (something is wrong: fees, slippage, or bug).
Phase 2 time exit: after 8 weeks regardless of P&L, a mandatory review week with execution halted.
Phase 2 entry requires all three gates: >=95% uptime over prior 4 weeks; at least one A/B-validated promotion (p<0.05, 100+ trades); positive 30-day P&L or explicit written override.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-04-26 — Three explicit gates for Phase 2 entry
hypotheses.resolution: Gate 1 (uptime) mechanically satisfied per 2026-07-02 entry; gates 2 and 3 not yet met.
